skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem: A sample of a certain alloy has a total mass of 60.0 grams and is 50.0% copper by mass. The sample was created by combining two pieces of different alloys. The first piece was 20.0% copper by mass and the second piece was 80.0% copper by mass. What was the mass, in grams, of the copper in the second piece?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Let a and b be the masses of the two pieces. Then a + b = 60 and 0.20a + 0.80b = 30 (50% of 60). Substituting a = 60 − b: 0.20(60 − b) + 0.80b = 30, so 12 + 0.60b = 30, giving b = 30. The second piece contains 0.80 × 30 = 24 grams of copper.
